long: 1 · Manual craft (do it well by hand)

themes.title: Triage & organise
notes: folders vs categories vs flags; rules on arrival
rungs: Suggest a folder-and-rule scheme for the mail you describe
"Summarise this thread — what's asked of me and by when?"
Script the folder + rule deployment from scratch

themes.title: Find & retrieve
rungs: Write the search-operator query for what you need
"Find the thread where the supplier confirmed the price change"
Build a mailbox report (senders, volumes, ageing) from an export

themes.title: Write & respond
notes: the survey's dominant cluster — most lessons live here
rungs: Reword this draft — tone, length, audience
Draft the reply from the whole thread's context
Generate a reply-template library from your recurring scenarios

themes.title: Schedule & coordinate
rungs: Draft the agenda and the invite text
Recap the meeting and draft the follow-up actions
Build the recurring meeting pack (agenda, minutes, invite) from scratch

themes.title: Automate the repetitive
notes: signatures, Quick Parts, Quick Steps, out-of-office
rungs: Turn a repeated email into reusable template text
Draft the out-of-office and standing replies in context
Script signature + template deployment from scratch

candidates.working_title: Folders, categories & flags: one home per mail
Rules that file mail on arrival

candidates.survey_signal: "find relevant emails, saving time searching archives"

candidates.working_title: Search Folders: saved searches that stay fresh
The one-ask email: subject lines & structure

candidates.survey_signal: DOMINANT: ~14 mentions — reword, formalise, professional standard, senior-leadership tone

candidates.working_title: From bullets to a formal reply
Invites that get accepted: agenda in the invite

candidates.survey_signal: "meeting notes to text"; "summarise meeting notes & populate minutes"

candidates.working_title: Quick Steps: one click, three actions
